title: Dolphin Watching Birthday Boat Hire in Puerto Banus: Costs 2026
html:
Dolphin watching birthday boat hire in Puerto Banus sets off from the marina jetties beside the Levante breakwater, where a private skippered trip to find bottlenose pods costs from about EUR 350 for a half day. BoatHire24 lists motorboats, ribs and catamarans for groups marking a birthday afloat.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus

The harbour is the obvious departure point, but the wider bay gives a birthday group several spots worth steering towards.

  • Puerto Jose Banus: the main marina, where most skippered birthday charters board.
  • Playa de Nueva Andalucia: the sand just west of the breakwater, calm at dawn.
  • Marbella old port: roughly 4 nautical miles east, a popular swim-stop cruise.
  • Cabopino: a smaller marina further east, quieter for an afternoon anchor.
  • San Pedro de Alcantara: the beachfront immediately west, good for a gentle out-and-back.

Why is Puerto Banus good for dolphin watching?

The Alboran Sea off Puerto Banus holds resident bottlenose and common dolphins year round, so sightings on a calm-water trip are frequent rather than rare. Pods follow the sardine and anchovy shoals along this stretch of the Costa del Sol. A skipper who knows the feeding lines often finds dorsal fins within the first hour. For flexible dates you can browse boats for your dates and match a sailing to your party size.

Which boat to rent in Puerto Banus

The right hull depends on group size, budget and whether you want speed to reach the pods or a stable deck for lingering. These options suit watching and a birthday alike.

  1. Open speedboat: fast, agile dolphin chasing for a small group; seats up to 8; from EUR 350 a half day. The Olympic 490 is a good example of this speedboat.
  2. Rib (rigid inflatable): spray and pace for an energetic party; up to 10 guests; from EUR 300.
  3. Motor cruiser: a roomy, shaded deck for a mixed-age group; up to 12 guests; from EUR 600 a half day.
  4. Sailing catamaran: the steadiest deck for relaxed watching and swimming; up to 12 guests; from EUR 900 a day.
  5. Crewed motor yacht: full service for a milestone; up to 12 guests; from EUR 1,800 a day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Puerto Banus?

Prices in Puerto Banus turn on boat size, season and whether skipper and fuel are included. The bands below give a realistic starting point for 2026.

  • A shared dolphin watching tour seat costs from about EUR 25 per person.
  • A small private motorboat half day starts near EUR 350.
  • A sailing catamaran full day runs from roughly EUR 900.
  • A crewed yacht day begins around EUR 1,800 before fuel and extras.

How does booking a birthday charter work?

You pick a boat and date online, confirm the skippered slot, then meet the crew at the marina on the day. A cake or a swim stop can be added in the booking notes, and the operator confirms what is included.

When is the best season, and do you need a licence?

The high season for Puerto Banus runs from June to September, when seas are calmest for spotting dolphins, though sightings continue all year. Conditions are usually most reliable early, before the afternoon breeze builds. Spain operates a licensing principle whereby some larger or faster craft require a qualified skipper, so many groups book a crewed boat, and the operator confirms the exact requirements for each listing.

Local tip: sail at first light to beat the Levante, the easterly wind that often kicks up a short chop along this coast by mid-afternoon and scatters the pods. Flat dawn water gives the steadiest watching and the best photographs of La Concha.

At a glance

  • Price band: from EUR 25 per person shared, EUR 350 for a private half day.
  • Peak months: June to September.
  • Trip length: half day around 4 hours, full day around 8 hours.
  • Capacity: typically up to 12 guests per boat.
  • Currency: EUR.

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus

Are dolphin sightings guaranteed on a birthday trip?

No charter can promise wildlife, but resident bottlenose and common dolphins are seen on most calm-weather trips off Puerto Banus, and an experienced skipper improves your odds in a half-day window.

How many people can join a birthday boat?

Most private charters here carry up to 12 guests, which suits a typical birthday group, and larger parties usually split across two boats or take a bigger crewed motor yacht.

Can we bring food and a cake on board?

Yes, most operators allow a cake, snacks and drinks for a birthday, though glass is sometimes restricted on lighter craft, so confirm the catering rules with the owner first.

How long does a dolphin watching trip last?

A standard half-day birthday charter runs about four hours, with time to find pods, anchor for a swim and celebrate, while a full day of roughly eight hours adds a longer cruise.

A skippered birthday afloat is a memorable way to mark a milestone on the Costa del Sol. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Puerto Banus or list your own, so browse the listings and compare crews and prices.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puerto Banus and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
